VERTOZ : FILES 4TH PATENT APPLICATION IN INDIA 🤖📡

• 📝 Vertoz has filed its 4th patent application in India on 24 June 2026

• 🔬 Patent titled: "System and Method for Demand Platform Optimization (DPO) Using Machine Learning-Based DSP Prediction, Ranking, and Real-Time Slot Allotment"

• 🤖 Technology uses machine learning to predict and rank advertising partners most likely to respond to a request

• ⚡ Aims to improve ad delivery efficiency by reducing unnecessary request forwarding and infrastructure overhead

• 📈 Expected to enable faster, more effective digital advertising transactions and enhance platform performance

• 💡 Strengthens Vertoz's intellectual property portfolio and reinforces its focus on AI-driven advertising technologies

🟢 Impact: Positive (Patent filing strengthens technology moat, supports innovation-led growth and enhances long-term competitive positioning.)

L&T : NCLT DIRECTS SHAREHOLDERS' MEETING FOR REALTY DEMERGER 🏗️

• ⚖️ NCLT Mumbai Bench has directed L&T to convene a shareholders' meeting through electronic mode

• 🔄 Meeting relates to the proposed Scheme of Arrangement between Larsen & Toubro Ltd (L&T) and L&T Realty Properties Ltd (LTRPL)

• 📅 Order dated June 16, 2026 (read with order dated June 12, 2026) has been received by the company

• 🗳️ Shareholders' approval will be sought for the proposed scheme within 60 days from the date of the order

• 📩 Company will finalize the meeting date and dispatch the notice to shareholders as required under the NCLT order

• 📝 L&T also clarified that a typographical error in the NCLT order is being rectified and does not affect the substance of the directions

🟢 Impact: Positive (Key regulatory milestone for the proposed restructuring/demerger process, moving the scheme closer to implementation.)

• Secured a strategic mandate from the Government of Assam to develop, launch and manage the state's official digital content platform.

• Platform will serve as a dedicated hub for regional entertainment, cultural programming, heritage content, films, music and live events.

Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) has received an order valued at ₹1,347.71 crore (gross) from Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL). 

The order encompasses Helina Launchers and LRUs valued at ₹1,109.37 crore, as well as CMDS LRUs valued at ₹238.34 crore. 

This contract was awarded by a domestic entity, HAL, with an execution period ranging between 24 and 60 months. 

Detailed contract terms remain confidential due to national security considerations.

Atmastco has secured an order valued at ₹57.33 crore from Adani Projects. 

The company received this order from L&T MHI Power Boilers Pvt Ltd. 

The scope of work encompasses the fabrication and supply of ceiling girders for the ICB-Boiler Area, totaling 4,368 metric tonnes.

INDIAN OVERSEAS DEATHS HEAVILY CONCENTRATED IN GULF COUNTRIES

• Government data shows Indian fatalities abroad are overwhelmingly concentrated in Gulf nations, with the UAE and Saudi Arabia accounting for the largest absolute number of deaths between 2021 and 2025.

• Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman and Kuwait recorded disproportionately high fatalities relative to the size of their Indian expatriate populations, while Qatar's mortality ratio remained comparatively lower.

• More than 37,700 Indian worker deaths were reported overseas during 2021–2025, with Gulf countries accounting for over 86% of the total.

ENDURANCE TECHNOLOGIES : TO SELL ITALY SOFTWARE SUBSIDIARY 🇮🇹💼

• 🔄 Endurance Overseas SpA (Italy), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Endurance Technologies, has signed an agreement to sell 100% stake in Veicoli Srl, Italy

• 💻 Veicoli is engaged in providing software platforms for the fleet management sector

• 🎯 Divestment aligns with Endurance Group's strategy to focus resources on its core auto components business

• 📅 Sale agreement signed on 23 June 2026; completion expected on 1 July 2026

• 💰 Deal consideration includes: • €2.2 million upfront payment • €0.3 million deferred payment • Earn-out linked to financial performance, capped at €5 million

• 📊 Veicoli contributed only 0.22% of FY26 consolidated income and 0.48% of consolidated net worth

• 🤝 Buyer is Banyan Software UK Limited; transaction is not a related-party deal

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive (Non-core asset monetization helps management sharpen focus on the auto components business, while financial impact remains limited due to Veicoli's small contribution.)

HINDUSTAN COPPER LIMITED: 

FOREST CLEARANCE GRANTED FOR CHANDMARI COPPER MINE IN RAJASTHAN; APPROVAL CO-TERMINUS WITH MINE LEASE PERIOD

Sri Priyanka Geo Commex IPO

The real story is not just revenue growth. It is working capital expansion.

The company’s working capital requirement is projected to rise from ₹17.27 crore in FY25 to ₹36.96 crore in FY27.

That is more than 2x in two years.

Using the FY23–FY25 average working capital turnover ratio of ~14.85x, this level of working capital can support revenue of nearly ₹549 crore in FY27.

For context, FY25 revenue was ₹266 crore.

So, the projected working capital base is implying almost 2x revenue potential.

Accenture raises buyback target to $7.5 billion; stock jumps in pre-market trade

Accenture shares climbed about 2% in pre-market trading on Tuesday after the global IT services company increased its fiscal 2026 share repurchase target by $2 billion, taking the total planned buyback to $7.5 billion.

The enhanced repurchase programme represents a 62% year-on-year increase and will be executed by Aug. 31, 2026, under the share repurchase authority approved by the company's Board of Directors in September 2025.

The additional $2 billion comes on top of the $300 million Accenture had already earmarked for repurchases during the current quarter, lifting its expected fourth-quarter buybacks to $2.3 billion.

reports.text:
Prostarm Info Systems

Order Win
- Secured ₹4.03 Cr contract from Karnataka State Fire and Emergency Services

Scope
- Supply, installation and commissioning of 3 KVA UPS systems
- Includes batteries compliant with IS 16242 standards

Execution
- Project completion timeline: 60 days

Client
- Karnataka State Fire and Emergency Services
- Government entity

Key Significance
- Strengthens presence in power backup solutions segment
- Adds to order book and execution pipeline
- No related party transactions involved

Impact
- Positive
- Enhances revenue visibility
- Reinforces position in government infrastructure projects

Redington

SSG Growth
- SSG revenue reached $2.2 Bn in FY26
- Contributed 17% of total revenue
- Grew 29% in USD terms and 37% in INR terms

Expansion Strategy
- Expanding across ASEAN, CIS and South Africa
- Cloud expansion targeting 30+ Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities in India

SSG Revenue Mix
- Software: 40%
- Cloud: 33%
- Security: 27%
- Services: <1%

Recurring Revenue Focus
- Recurring revenue increased to 74% in FY26
- Targeting 85% over the next 3 years
- Renewal rate at 85%
- Targeting 90% renewal rate

AI & Cloud Focus
- Microsoft Frontier partner
- AWS Generative AI competency
- AI Exchange platform launched with 450+ agents
- Academy focused on partner upskilling

Key Partners
- Microsoft
- AWS
- Autodesk
- Red Hat
- Fortinet
- Palo Alto Networks

Margin Profile
- Professional services margins above 20%
- SSG margin target: 5.5%–6%
- PAT margin target: ~2%

Long-Term Vision
- SSG revenue contribution target: 25%
- Current contribution: 17%
- Target SSG revenue: $5 Bn

Impact
- Positive
- Strong cloud, cybersecurity and AI growth trajectory
- Increasing recurring revenue improves business quality
- Expansion into emerging markets supports long-term growth visibility

Honasa Consumer

Strategic Acquisition
- Acquired 58% stake in Fluence Pharma
- Enters the nutraceuticals market
- Established Honasa Health Pvt Ltd as a new subsidiary

About Fluence Pharma
- FY26 Revenue: ~₹40 Cr
- EBITDA Margin: 20%+
- Proprietary Cyclical Nutrition Therapy (CNT) platform
- Network of 3,000+ dermatologists

Market Opportunity
- Targets nutraceutical market opportunity of ₹16,000 Cr+
- Expands Honasa beyond beauty and personal care

Leadership
- Honasa Health to be led by Dheeraj Nagpal
- Fluence co-founded by Amit Bhusari and Dr Rajendra Singh Rajput

Strategic Significance
- Expands presence into health and wellness segment
- Creates cross-selling opportunities across existing brands
- Leverages dermatology-led distribution network
- Adds a high-margin growth category

Existing Brand Portfolio
- Mamaearth
- The Derma Co.
- Aqualogica
- BBlunt
- Dr Sheth's
- Staze

Impact
- Positive
- Entry into fast-growing nutraceutical segment
- Diversifies revenue streams beyond personal care
- Strengthens health and wellness ecosystem

Shaily Engineering Plastics

GLP-1 Opportunity
- Management remains positive on injectable GLP-1 therapies
- Views injectables as superior to oral alternatives
- Cites higher efficacy and lower API consumption requirements

Pen Capacity Expansion
- Annual pen manufacturing capacity increased from ~25 Mn to ~55 Mn units
- Additional ~25 Mn units capacity to be commissioned in FY27
- Total installed capacity expected to reach ~80-85 Mn units

Demand Visibility
- Around 60-65% of upcoming capacity already backed by customer commitments
- Provides strong revenue visibility for future growth

New Growth Avenue
- Semiconductor packaging consumables identified as a strategic opportunity
- Focus on chip trays and related products

Impact
- Positive
- Strong capacity expansion supported by customer demand
- GLP-1 delivery devices remain a key growth driver
- Semiconductor consumables offer an additional long-term growth opportunity

WEAK MONSOON IMPACT

Agri Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan Says

Monsoon Delayed Due To El Niño, Overall Rains 43% Lower Till Now

Monsoon Likely To Continue To Be Weak Till July 2nd Week & Could Impact Kharif Crop

111 Districts Are High Priority As Sowing Has Been Less Than 25%, 22 Of These Districts In Maharashtra

12 States Including MP, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, UP, Rajasthan & Gujarat Likely To Bear Higher Impact Of Less Rains

States Can Adopt Crops Which Are Less Water Consuming

Infosys: GlobalFoundries Partnership Expanded

Key Highlights
- Multi-year deal expanded
- AI-led transformation focus
- Managed services model
- IT operations modernization

Scope of Work
- Application management
- Infrastructure services
- Data operations
- Service desk support

Strategic Impact
- Higher AI adoption
- Automation-led efficiency
- Better user experience
- Deepens client relationship

Key Takeaway
- Strengthens large-client engagement
- Expands managed services role
- Reinforces AI capabilities
Solarium Green Energy Ltd – Wins ₹186.53 Cr Solar Project

U.S. PRESIDENT TRUMP SAYS

Iran released funds to be used for food and essential supplies from the US

Purchases will be made exclusively from the US

Funds will be held in a US-controlled escrow account

Iran urgently needs food and medical supplies

Iran has agreed to nuclear inspections
THE RBI WAS FORCED TO PUMP A STAGGERING ₹1.41 LAKH CRORE INTO BANKS TO PREVENT A SUDDEN CASH CRUNCH. AS GST PAYMENTS DRAIN LIQUIDITY FROM THE MARKET

Shyam Dhani Industries

Growth Target
- Targets ₹300 Cr turnover within 3 years
- FY26 Turnover: ₹146.22 Cr
- FY26 Growth: 17% YoY
- Target Growth Rate: 25-30%

Key Growth Drivers
- Retail expansion across India
- E-commerce growth
- Export expansion to China, Middle East and Europe
- Quality enhancement through Jaipur processing facility

Strategic Focus
- Scale domestic retail presence
- Expand export business
- Invest in manufacturing infrastructure
- Focus on premium spice products

Impact
- Positive
- Ambitious growth roadmap
- Focused on domestic and export expansion

Power & Instrumentation (Gujarat)

Additional Order
- Received additional order worth ₹3.72 Cr
- Awarded by Ajmer Vidyut Vitran Nigam Ltd

Scope
- Supply, erection, installation, testing and commissioning
- 11 KV feeder segregation under RDSS scheme
- Turnkey execution basis

Order Book
- Total aggregated order value: ₹38.28 Cr

Execution
- Timeline: 15 months

Impact
- Positive
- Strengthens order book visibility
- Expands utility infrastructure presence

Supreme Power Equipment

Order Win
- Secured domestic order worth ₹5.60 Cr
- Received from a New Delhi-based EPC company

Scope
- Supply of 20 MVA, 66/11 KV power transformers
- Delivery location: Karnataka

Execution
- Timeline: Approximately 11 months

Impact
- Positive
- Adds to order book
- Improves revenue visibility

NTPC

Patratu STPP Update
- Unit-2 (800 MW) commenced commercial operations
- Effective from June 25, 2026
- Developed through PVUNL subsidiary

Capacity Update
- Patratu Phase-1 capacity: 3x800 MW
- NTPC Group installed capacity: 90,857 MW
- Commercial capacity: 89,777 MW

Impact
- Positive
- Expands generation capacity
- Supports long-term revenue growth

PFC

Fund Raising
- Raised US$300 Mn through Global Medium Term Notes
- Coupon Rate: 5.32%
- Maturity: June 30, 2031

Key Details
- Issued under US$8 Bn GMTN Programme
- Interest payable semi-annually in US Dollars
- Notes to be listed on NSE IFSC and India INX

Impact
- Positive
- Strengthens funding profile
- Supports future lending growth

HCLTECH

AI-Led Network Operations Partnership
- Collaborating with Circles & GreySkies
- Developing AI-powered telecom operations framework
- Enables predictive issue detection and autonomous decision-making
- Integrates platforms using TM Forum Open APIs

Impact
- Positive
- Enhances AI and telecom automation capabilities

NLC INDIA

Renewable Energy JV
- Signed MoU with IOCL
- Focus on Solar, Wind and Hybrid projects
- Plans for Battery Energy Storage and Pumped Storage
- Power supply targeted at C&I, Discoms and e-mobility sectors
- Exploring green fuels and power-to-X opportunities

Impact
- Positive
- Strengthens renewable energy growth pipeline

RUDRA GAS ENTERPRISE

Bio Fuels Expansion
- Incorporated Rudra Bio Energy Pvt Ltd
- Entering bio fuels manufacturing, processing and distribution
- Invested ₹51,000 for 51% stake
- No related party involvement

Impact
- Positive
- Diversifies business operations
- Creates a new growth avenue

WIPRO

AI-Driven Cybersecurity Partnership
- Expanded partnership with Palo Alto Networks
- Integrates Cortex XSIAM with Wipro CyberShield
- Enhances threat detection and incident response
- Uses AI, machine learning and automation
- Supported by WEGA and WINGS AI platforms

Impact
- Positive
- Strengthens cybersecurity and AI service offerings

LT Foods Ltd – Australia Subsidiary Incorporated 🌏
LT Foods Ltd has incorporated a new wholly owned subsidiary, LT Foods Australia Pty Limited, in Australia.
• Subsidiary Name: LT Foods Australia Pty Limited
• Country: Australia
• Ownership: 100% Wholly Owned Subsidiary
• Incorporation Date: June 23, 2026
• Purpose: Strengthen LT Foods' international presence and support global growth initiatives
• This follows the company's earlier disclosure dated June 15, 2026 regarding the proposed incorporation.
• The move marks a strategic expansion into the Australian market and reinforces LT Foods' global growth strategy.

Sarvam AI

Major Milestone
- Became India's first AI unicorn
- Demonstrates India's capability to build foundation AI companies

Funding Challenge
- Sarvam AI recently raised ~$234 Mn
- China's DeepSeek reportedly secured ~$7.4 Bn
- Highlights the large capital gap in global AI competition

India vs Global AI Funding
- India attracted ~$11.1 Bn private AI investment (2013-2024)
- US invested ~$285.9 Bn in AI in 2025 alone
- Capital availability remains India's biggest scaling challenge

Why Foundation AI Is Different
- Requires massive spending on:
  - GPUs and compute infrastructure
  - Proprietary datasets
  - AI research talent
  - Model training and deployment
- Funding needs can run into billions of dollars

Key Ecosystem Gap
- India has strong AI talent and entrepreneurs
- Early-stage funding ecosystem is improving
- Missing the large-scale, patient capital needed for global AI leaders

Industry View
- Foundation AI should be treated as strategic infrastructure
- Commercialization cycles are much longer than traditional software
- Companies may require multiple funding rounds before meaningful revenues emerge

What India Needs
- Larger deeptech-focused capital pools
- Long-term investors willing to support multi-year development cycles
- Stronger scale-up funding ecosystem between startup and global leadership stage

Key Takeaway
- Sarvam AI's success proves India can build ambitious foundation-model companies.
- The next challenge is not innovation, but securing the patient, multi-billion-dollar capital required to scale globally.
- Without a stronger scale-up funding ecosystem, India risks creating AI innovators that struggle to compete with heavily funded global rivals.

**Jefferies on Insurance Sector**

**Regulatory Update**
- IRDAI has released five exposure drafts
- Covers investments, intermediaries, TPAs, reinsurers and penalties
- Signals ongoing regulatory framework refinement

**Key Readthroughs for Insurers**

**Investment Flexibility**
- Life insurers may be allowed to undertake Repo/Government Lending transactions
- Permitted up to 10% of available G-Sec holdings
- Could improve investment portfolio efficiency
- Enhances liquidity management flexibility

**Intermediary Disclosure Norms**
- Corporate agents earning commissions above ₹10 Cr annually will be required to disclose commissions every year
- Aims to improve transparency in distribution practices
- Could impact large insurance distribution platforms

**Implications for PB Fintech**
- Disclosure requirements may increase transparency around commission structures
- Relevant for large-scale insurance distribution businesses
- Investors likely to monitor implementation details

**Commission Regulations**
- Commission framework remains linked to EOM Regulations 2024
- No exposure draft released yet on this specific aspect
- Further clarity awaited from the regulator

**Sector Impact**
- Improved investment flexibility for life insurers
- Greater transparency across insurance distribution ecosystem
- Regulatory environment continues to evolve
- No immediate material earnings impact indicated

**Key Monitorables**
- Final IRDAI regulations after consultation process
- Details of EOM Regulations 2024
- Implementation timeline for commission disclosures
- Impact of repo and government lending provisions on insurer investment income

**Key Takeaway**
- The most significant proposal is allowing life insurers to deploy up to 10% of their available G-Sec holdings in repo/government lending transactions, potentially improving investment efficiency.
- Enhanced disclosure requirements for corporate agents earning over ₹10 Cr in commissions could increase transparency for insurance distributors, including platforms such as PB Fintech.

- Trading at approximately 11x FY28CL earnings
- CLSA views valuation as inexpensive relative to growth potential

**Key Positives**
- Strong Z5 user growth
- Most downloaded OTT platform
- Long-term FIFA content rights secured
- Improving TV market share
- Attractive valuation

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA believes Zee Entertainment is benefiting from strong FIFA World Cup-driven engagement, with Z5 weekly active users doubling and becoming the most downloaded OTT platform.
- The company's long-term FIFA rights through 2034, improving television market share and inexpensive valuation at 1x sales and 11x FY28 earnings support a positive outlook.

**CLSA on IDFC First Bank**

**Rating & Target Price**
- Hold
- Target Price: ₹73/share

**Growth Outlook**
- Management continues to guide for ~20% loan growth in FY27
- Deposit growth also guided at ~20%
- Growth momentum remains strong across businesses

**Deposit Strategy**
- Savings account and term deposit rates adjusted across buckets
- Changes not expected to materially impact cost of deposits
- Funding profile remains stable

**Margin Outlook**
- Management reiterated FY27 NIM guidance of 5.75%
- Deposit repricing impact expected to be limited
- Margin outlook remains stable

**Asset Quality**
- Asset quality continues to remain strong
- Non-MFI segments delivering lower slippages
- Credit performance remains healthy across portfolios

**Profitability Outlook**
- Long-term RoA target: 1.6-1.7%
- Improvement expected through operating leverage
- Scale benefits expected to support profitability expansion

**Capital Position**
- Tier I Capital Ratio: 13.7%
- Capital position remains adequate for growth
- Strong growth outlook could necessitate capital raising

**Capital Raise Outlook**
- Management indicated focus on sustaining growth
- Given growth trajectory and capital levels, a capital raise is likely in the near term

**Key Positives**
- Strong loan growth visibility
- Healthy deposit growth
- Stable NIM guidance
- Improving operating leverage
- Strong asset quality trends

**Key Monitorables**
- Capital raising plans
- NIM performance against 5.75% guidance
- Deposit growth momentum
- Asset quality trends
- RoA improvement trajectory

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA remains constructive on IDFC First Bank's growth and asset quality outlook, with management maintaining guidance of ~20% loan and deposit growth and 5.75% NIM for FY27.
- Strong operating leverage is expected to drive long-term RoA improvement, while robust growth may require a capital raise in the near future.

**Jefferies on NBFC Sector**

**Sector View**
- Outlook has improved following easing of West Asia tensions
- Operating environment becoming more supportive
- Sector fundamentals remain healthy

**Business Trends**
- Loan growth tracking ahead of last year
- Collection efficiency remains strong
- Asset quality trends remain stable
- Credit performance improving across segments

**Margin Outlook**
- Softer bond yields supportive for funding costs
- Delayed rate-hike expectations beneficial
- NIMs expected to remain range-bound
- Funding environment gradually improving

**Profitability Outlook**
- Healthy growth expected across the sector
- Credit costs likely to ease
- Stable margins support earnings visibility
- Improved operating environment aids profitability

**Valuation View**
- Valuations have recovered from post-conflict lows
- Sector valuations now near historical averages
- No longer deeply discounted
- Still supported by improving fundamentals

**Key Risk**
- Weak monsoon
- Potential El Niño impact
- Rural demand slowdown could affect certain lending segments
- Collections and growth may be impacted if monsoon disappoints

**Preferred Segment**
- Diversified NBFCs preferred in the near term
- Better positioned to navigate changing macro conditions
- More balanced growth and risk profile

**Top Picks**

**Bajaj Finance (BAF)**
- Preferred diversified NBFC
- Strong growth and execution track record

**Aditya Birla Capital (ABCAP)**
- Diversified financial services platform
- Well positioned for improving credit cycle

**Cholamandalam Investment & Finance (CIFC)**
- Strong asset quality and growth profile
- Beneficiary of improving operating environment

**Shriram Finance (SHFL)**
- Diversified lending franchise
- Attractive positioning in current cycle

**Key Takeaway**
- Jefferies believes the NBFC sector outlook has improved as geopolitical concerns ease, funding conditions become more supportive and collections remain healthy.
- Diversified NBFCs are preferred due to their ability to deliver healthy growth, stable margins and easing credit costs while managing macro uncertainties.

**Jefferies on Emcure Pharma**

**Rating & Target Price**
- Buy
- Target Price Raised to ₹2,100/share

**FY27 Outlook**
- Revenue growth expected in low to mid-teens
- EBITDA margin expansion of 75-100 bps anticipated
- Growth outlook remains healthy across key businesses

**Margin Expansion Drivers**
- Higher field force productivity in India
- Improved operating leverage
- New product launches in key global markets
- Better business mix and execution

**Growth Strategy**
- R&D-led product portfolio remains the core focus
- Innovation-driven growth approach
- Continued emphasis on differentiated products
- Focus on sustainable long-term expansion

**Earnings Outlook**
- Expected EPS CAGR of ~20% over FY26-29E
- Consistent earnings growth visibility
- Supported by revenue growth and margin expansion

**Valuation**
- FY27E P/E: 29x
- FY28E P/E: 24x
- Jefferies considers valuation attractive relative to growth profile

**Key Growth Drivers**
- Strong India business execution
- Productivity improvements in domestic operations
- New launches in international markets
- R&D-backed product pipeline
- Margin expansion through operating efficiencies

**Key Takeaway**
- Jefferies believes Emcure Pharma is well-positioned to deliver low-to-mid teens revenue growth and 75-100 bps margin expansion in FY27.
- Long-term growth remains driven by R&D-backed products, while a projected 20% EPS CAGR over FY26-29E supports the positive investment outlook.

**Jefferies on Blue Star**

**Rating & Target Price**
- Hold
- Target Price: ₹1,735/share

**Room Air Conditioner (RAC) Industry**
- Apr-May 2026 industry sales growth estimated at 25-30% YoY
- Strong summer season driving demand
- Industry demand environment remains healthy

**Data Center Opportunity**

**Industry Potential**
- India's data center capacity estimated at 1.5 GW currently
- Capacity expected to grow around 5x by 2030E
- Creates a large infrastructure opportunity

**Blue Star Positioning**
- Data centers identified as a major long-term growth driver
- Strong presence in MEP and cooling solutions
- Well positioned to benefit from rising data center investments

**Addressable Market**
- Estimated TAM equivalent to nearly 50% of total data center capex
- Opportunity includes:
  - MEP (Mechanical, Electrical & Plumbing)
  - Cooling infrastructure

**Growth Outlook**
- Current DC-MEP revenue: ~₹1,000 Cr
- Represents ~10% of total sales currently
- Expected to reach ~₹3,000 Cr over next 3-4 years
- Revenue contribution could increase to ~15% of total sales

**Key Growth Drivers**
- Hyperscale data center expansion
- AI infrastructure investments
- Rising cloud adoption
- Increasing demand for cooling solutions
- Growth in digital infrastructure

**Investment View**
- RAC business continues to benefit from strong demand
- Data centers provide a multi-year growth runway
- Cooling and MEP capabilities position the company well for future opportunities

**Key Takeaway**
- Jefferies sees data centers as one of Blue Star's most significant long-term growth opportunities.
- With India's data center capacity expected to grow nearly fivefold by 2030E, Blue Star aims to triple its DC-MEP revenues from ~₹1,000 Cr to ~₹3,000 Cr over the next 3-4 years, increasing its contribution from 10% to 15% of total sales.

**Jefferies on Power T&D Sector**

**Sector View**
- Positive outlook on Power Transmission & Distribution equipment
- Strong growth expected during FY26E-FY30E
- Driven by large transmission infrastructure projects
- Revenue visibility remains robust across the sector

**Data Center Opportunity**
- Data centers emerging as an additional demand driver
- Approximately 30% of data center capex linked to power T&D equipment
- Rising AI and hyperscale investments expected to support demand
- Creates a long-term growth runway for equipment suppliers

**Preferred Picks**

**Hitachi Energy India**
- Buy maintained
- Strong revenue visibility
- Expected to benefit from transmission capex cycle
- Supported by operating leverage

**Siemens Energy India**
- Buy maintained
- Strong order inflow outlook
- Beneficiary of power infrastructure investments
- Operating leverage expected to drive earnings growth

**Earnings Outlook**
- Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy expected to deliver 40%+ earnings CAGR
- Growth driven by:
  - Strong order books
  - Revenue visibility
  - Operating leverage benefits
  - Transmission sector expansion

**GE Vernova T&D**
- Initiated with Hold rating
- Target Price: ₹6,000/share

**Key Sector Drivers**
- National transmission network expansion
- Renewable energy integration
- Grid modernization
- Data center investments
- Rising power demand
- Industrial electrification

**Key Takeaway**
- Jefferies remains constructive on the Power T&D sector given strong transmission project activity and incremental demand from data centers.
- Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy remain preferred plays due to superior earnings growth visibility and operating leverage.
- GE Vernova T&D is initiated with a Hold rating despite favorable sector tailwinds.

**Citi on Cement Sector**

**Industry Outlook FY27**
- Industry utilization expected to decline slightly to ~66%
- Assumes demand growth of 6%
- EBITDA per tonne likely to decline YoY
- Near-term profitability expected to remain under pressure

**Why FY27 Could Remain Challenging**
- Capacity additions continue across the industry
- Demand growth may not fully absorb new capacity
- Utilization levels expected to soften
- Profitability likely to moderate

**FY28 Outlook**
- Expected to be a stronger year for the sector
- Profitability outlook improves significantly
- Industry dynamics expected to become more favorable

**Key Drivers for FY28 Recovery**

**Capacity Expansion Timing**
- Around 80% of planned capacity additions expected in 2H
- Capacity additions are back-ended
- Better absorption expected over time

**Demand Growth**
- Assumes 7% industry demand growth
- Utilization expected to improve versus FY27
- Higher utilization should support profitability

**Margin Outlook**
- EBITDA per tonne expected to expand in FY28
- Improved operating leverage likely
- Better industry utilization supports margins

**Top Picks**

**UltraTech Cement**
- Preferred large-cap pick
- EV/Tonne: $150
- Based on expanded capacity

**JSW Cement**
- Preferred small/mid-cap pick
- EV/Tonne: $70

**Key Takeaway**
- FY27 may see slightly weaker utilization and profitability.
- FY28 is expected to be a stronger year as demand growth improves utilization and supports EBITDA per tonne expansion.
- UltraTech Cement remains Citi's preferred large-cap idea, while JSW Cement is its preferred small/mid-cap play.

**Artificial Intelligence Strategy**

**Revenue Outlook**

**Margin Expansion Story**

**Earnings Growth Outlook**

**Key Growth Drivers**

- Operating leverage from scaling platform revenues

**Key Monitorables**
- Execution of AI strategy
- Margin expansion progress
- Growth in international customer base
- Streaming industry spending trends
- Seasonal revenue performance

**Investment Thesis**
- Strong structural growth opportunity
- High-quality SaaS business model
- Industry-leading growth profile
- Significant operating leverage potential
- AI initiatives could create additional long-term opportunities

**Impact**
- Positive
- Strong revenue growth visibility
- Margin expansion story remains intact
- AI investments support future competitiveness
- One of the faster-growing media technology platforms globally

Emkay raises Stovekraft TP by 29% to ₹900 vs ₹700

Why the re-rating?

✅ Govt electrification push benefiting organized players

✅ Appliance demand recovery underway, emerging from a post-Covid 3 year demand slowdown

✅ Exports scaling via IKEA & Walmart, last 2 years hit by war & tariff disruptions

✅ Capex cycle complete; existing capacity can drive ~2x revenue

Forecasts FY26-28E CAGR: Revenue +18%, EBITDA +24%, EPS +52%.

**Permanent Magnets**

**Business Transformation Story**
- FY26 Revenue: ₹225 Cr
- Transitioning beyond traditional magnet business
- Building three new growth engines
- FY27 could mark the start of commercialization

**Growth Engine 1: Alloys Division**

**Current Status**
- New furnace commissioned in Jan'26
- Utilization reached 80-90% by FY26-end
- Major contributor to strong Q4 growth
- Commercial production already underway

**Growth Outlook**
- Demand emerging from oil & gas sector
- FY27 revenue expected to be 3-4x FY26 levels
- First growth engine already operational
- Immediate contributor to earnings growth

**Growth Engine 2: Relays Business**

**Opportunity**
- Linked to India's smart meter rollout
- Relay content value: ₹250-300 per meter
- Addressable market: ~25 crore smart meters
- Large long-term domestic opportunity

**FY27 Outlook**
- Commercialization expected in H2FY27
- Customer approvals and pilot testing underway
- Potential revenue opportunity: ₹25-50 Cr
- Emerging smart infrastructure play

**Growth Engine 3: Quantum Magnetics**

**Strategic Importance**
- Focused on rare earth magnets
- Beneficiary of global supply chain diversification
- Positioned amid China's export restrictions
- Addresses strategic manufacturing opportunity

**Project Progress**
- Phase 1 implemented
- Phase 2 under development
- Block cutting, machining and surface treatment planned in FY27
- First commercial revenues expected during FY27

**Long-Term Vision**
- Target: 5,000-tonne rare earth magnet ecosystem
- Potential revenue opportunity: ₹3,000-4,000 Cr
- More than 10x current company revenue
- Multi-year execution roadmap

**Why It Matters**
- Rare earth magnets critical for:
  - Electric Vehicles
  - Defence Systems
  - Wind Turbines
  - Industrial Automation
  - Electronics Manufacturing

**Key FY27 Triggers**
- Alloys division scaling up
- Relay business commercialization
- Quantum Magnetics revenue commencement
- Transition from investment phase to execution phase

**Key Risks**
- Commercialization delays
- Customer qualification timelines
- Execution of rare earth expansion plans
- Dependence on approvals and scaling success

**Investment Thesis**
- Permanent Magnets is evolving from a niche magnet manufacturer into a diversified industrial technology company.
- The Alloys business provides near-term growth, Relays offer exposure to India's smart metering rollout, while Quantum Magnetics represents a potentially transformational long-term opportunity.

**Key Takeaway**
- For several years, the story was about investments and capacity creation.
- FY27 could become the inflection year where Alloys, Relays and Quantum Magnetics begin contributing commercially.
- The biggest long-term opportunity remains the rare earth magnet business, but successful execution of all three growth engines will determine whether the company can evolve into a much larger industrial platform over the next decade.

**Trent: Axis PUNCH BUY Report**

**Recommendation**
- BUY rating maintained
- Target Price: ₹3,340
- CMP: ₹3,035
- Upside potential: ~10%
- Investment horizon: 3-6 months

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue growth: 20.2% YoY
- EBITDA growth: 40% YoY
- Online sales growth: 25% YoY
- Continued strong operating performance

**Store Expansion**
- Westside added 52 stores in FY26
- Westside network reached 300 stores
- Zudio added 198 stores during FY26
- Zudio network expanded to 963 stores
- Total store count reached 1,286

**Growth Drivers**
- Zudio remains the key growth engine
- Expansion into Tier II and III cities
- Improving cluster productivity
- Strong lifestyle and fashion demand

**Management Outlook**
- Positive on long-term growth prospects
- Continued store expansion planned
- Healthy margin outlook
- Focus on scaling lifestyle brands

**Macro Tailwinds**
- Lower crude prices
- Moderating inflation
- Potential GST reforms
- Improving discretionary spending environment

**Key Risks**
- Rising competition from online retailers
- Pressure on consumer spending
- Increased competitive intensity
- Execution risk in rapid expansion

**Key Takeaway**
- Trent continues to be one of India's strongest retail growth stories
- Zudio expansion remains the primary growth catalyst
- Strong execution, store additions and improving profitability support long-term growth visibility

**Gravita India: Axis PUNCH BUY Report**

**Recommendation**
- BUY rating maintained
- Target Price: ₹1,900

**Copper Expansion Strategy**

**Growth Guidance**

**Key Growth Drivers**
- Copper recycling business

- Capacity additions
- Lithium-ion battery recycling
- Value-added products expansion

**Key Risks**
- Delay in project execution
- Raw material price volatility
- Product price fluctuations
- Slower-than-expected capacity ramp-up

**Key Takeaway**
- Gravita is transforming into a diversified recycling platform
- Copper business could become the next major growth driver
- Strong capex pipeline and earnings guidance support long-term growth visibility
